idea-plugin.name: Hot Path Heatmap
idea-plugin.description:
Static hot path heatmap for PHP and JavaScript/TypeScript.

Why? In source, every call reads as equally cheap: $user->getId() looks exactly like $user->calculateTheMeaningOfLife() — yet one is a trivial field access and the other may fan out through services, loops, and a repository. Hot Path Heatmap was built to make that difference visible at a glance, so you notice the expensive call before it becomes a production problem.

It highlights method call sites that may trigger expensive downstream execution paths — deep call chains, nested loops, repository / HTTP-client access, high fan-out — by statically composing cached method summaries from the IDE's PSI and indexes.

Each flagged call gets a severity-colored outline, a cost score in the gutter, and a tooltip explaining why. This is a static estimate, not a runtime profiler — it exists to give an early visual warning: "this innocent-looking line may be expensive."
idea-plugin.change-notes:
0.1.4
  • Exclusion now follows the IDE's own directory marks instead of matching path strings: folders marked Excluded and Test Sources Root (via Mark Directory as…) are skipped by default, each toggleable in settings. Built-in/library code is always skipped.
  • Aligned the toolbar flame icon with the Run/Debug controls.
0.1.3
  • Added a show/hide toggle button to the main toolbar (next to Run/Debug), also available in the Tools and editor right-click menus. A lit flame means the heatmap is on; a slashed flame means it is off.
  • While hidden, no cost scores are calculated at all.
  • Expanded the plugin description with the motivation behind it.
0.1.2
  • Removed the upper IDE-compatibility bound so the plugin installs on 2024.2 and all future PhpStorm builds. Verified compatible against the 2024.2 and 2025.3 (253) lines.
0.1.1
  • Listing metadata: license, vendor, and description updates.
0.1.0
  • Initial release.
  • Static hot-path heatmap for PHP and JavaScript/TypeScript.
  • Severity-colored marker, gutter cost score, and an explanatory tooltip.
  • Configurable heuristics, traversal limits, and vendor/test exclusion.

action.description: Show or hide the hot path heatmap. While hidden, no cost scores are calculated.
